title: Shortest Distance in a Line
description: Find the shortest distance between any two points on a line.
constraints: x is the primary key — all x values are unique
There are at least 2 points (so a minimum distance always exists)
key_concept: Self-join on p1.x < p2.x, compute p2.x - p1.x for all pairs, take MIN.

hints.L1: Self-join and compute differences. Use x < other_x to avoid duplicates.
hints.L2: SELECT MIN(p2.x - p1.x) AS shortest FROM Point p1 JOIN Point p2 ON p1.x < p2.x
hints.L3: SELECT MIN(p2.x - p1.x) AS shortest FROM Point p1 JOIN Point p2 ON p2.x > p1.x

optimal_solution: SELECT MIN(p2.x - p1.x) AS shortest FROM Point p1 JOIN Point p2 ON p2.x > p1.x;
